basic_info.title: Metal Gear
basic_info.review: A groundbreaking title that defined the stealth genre, offering a unique blend of action and strategy on the MSX2.

play_today.details: Modern PC port with widescreen support, save states, and no region lock. Best for accessibility and ease of play.

recommended_patches.name: Metal Gear Solid HD Collection Patch
recommended_patches.description: Enhanced graphics and widescreen support from the HD Collection port

play_today.platform: Nintendo Switch / PlayStation 4 / PlayStation 5 / Xbox Series X/S
play_today.details: Metal Gear Solid: Master Collection Vol. 1 - Official compilation with both MSX2 and NES versions, modernized controls.
Original canonical version by Hideo Kojima. Requires MSX emulator with tape drive support for save functionality.

recommended_patches.name: MSX2 Improvements Patch
recommended_patches.description: Dynamic Vsync, turbo speed controls, improved graphics for bridge parts, removes region lock

play_today.details: Original Western release with significant changes from Kojima's vision. Includes fan translation patches for improved localization.

recommended_patches.name: Personal Area Network Patch
recommended_patches.description: Automatically opens doors when you have the correct card key - quality of life improvement

play_today.details: 1989 port based on NES version. Limited availability but historically significant.

The original PC port offers a unique experience with mouse support and different control schemes compared to console versions. While graphics are lower resolution and frame rates vary, it provides the most authentic 1990s PC gaming experience. Best for purists seeking the original DOS environment without modern emulation layers.

recommended_patches.name: Metal Gear MSX2 English Translation Patch
recommended_patches.description: A fan translation patch that converts the Japanese MSX2 version of Metal Gear into English, fixing the 'Engrish' of the European port and restoring cut messages.

recommended_patches.name: MSX2+ High Resolution Patch
recommended_patches.description: Allows the MSX2 version of Metal Gear to run at higher resolutions on modern MSX emulators, improving clarity on large screens.

optimal_settings: Set CPU speed to 'Turbo R' for maximum performance
Enable 'VDP' scaling to 2x or 4x for crisp graphics
Configure audio to 'MSX-Audio' for enhanced sound effects
Set CPU to 'Turbo R' for smooth gameplay
Enable 'Screen scaling' to 2x for better visibility
Configure touch controls for D-pad and action buttons

play_today.details: The definitive modern experience via Steam's Master Collection. Features native widescreen support, controller remapping, and cloud saves. While the base emulation is criticized for lack of enhancements, the PC version allows community mods for QoL improvements, higher resolutions, and bug fixes not present in console versions. Best for accessibility and modding.

recommended_patches.name: MGSHDFix
recommended_patches.description: A comprehensive fix for the Metal Gear Solid Master Collection on PC that enables native widescreen support, custom resolutions, and fixes various emulation bugs present in the base game.

recommended_patches.name: MGS2MC Better Audio
recommended_patches.description: Restores the original high-quality audio tracks for Metal Gear Solid 2 in the Master Collection, replacing the compressed audio files with the original source audio.

recommended_patches.name: MGS2 High Resolution Textures (AI Slop Edition)
recommended_patches.description: Upscales the textures in Metal Gear Solid 2 to 4x resolution using AI upscaling for a sharper visual experience on modern displays.

recommended_patches.name: MGSM2Fix
recommended_patches.description: A collection of fixes for Metal Gear Solid 2 in the Master Collection, addressing specific graphical glitches and performance issues.

play_today.details: The original NES port is a 2D side-scrolling action game, vastly different from the 3D stealth gameplay of the PS1 version. It is historically significant but lacks the depth and mechanics of later entries. Recommended only for completionists or those interested in the series' 2D origins. Not recommended for a first-time experience of the Metal Gear Solid franchise.

recommended_patches.name: Metal Gear (NES) Bugfix and Style Patch
recommended_patches.description: Corrects numerous spelling, grammatical, and punctuation errors in the NES version, and fixes gameplay errors such as the incorrect location for the EOD suit.

recommended_patches.name: Metal Gear (NES) Uncensoring Patch
recommended_patches.description: Restores censored content and fixes minor graphical glitches in the NES version of Metal Gear.

optimal_settings: Touch Controls: Enable on-screen D-pad and buttons

description.synopsis: In 1995, the player controls Solid Snake, a special forces operative sent to infiltrate Outer Heaven, a fortress nation in Africa. His mission is to stop the rogue commander Big Boss and destroy the Metal Gear, a walking nuclear tank capable of launching missiles from anywhere. Snake must navigate a heavily guarded facility using stealth, avoiding detection while uncovering a deeper conspiracy.
key_features: Radio communication system for intel and hints
Inventory management with limited carrying capacity
Use of environmental cover and shadows
Multiple endings based on player choices
Boss battles against mechs and special units
Real-time movement with turn-based combat elements
legacy_and_impact: Metal Gear is widely credited with creating the stealth-action genre, shifting the focus of video game combat from direct confrontation to evasion and strategy. Its narrative depth and political themes set a new standard for storytelling in video games, influencing countless titles that followed.
